back_translation: When the LORD was about to take Elijah up to heaven in a whirlwind, Elijah and Elisha were on their way from Gilgal.

Elijah said to Elisha, “Please stay here, for the LORD has sent me to Bethel.” But Elisha replied, “As the LORD lives and as you yourself live, I will never leave you.” So the two of them went down to Bethel.
The disciples in the company of the prophets at Bethel came out to Elisha and asked, “Do you know that today the LORD will take your master away from you?” Elisha replied, “Yes, I know; be quiet.”
Elijah said to him, “Elisha, please stay here, for the LORD has sent me to Jericho.” But Elisha replied, “As the LORD lives and as you yourself live, I will never leave you.” So they came to Jericho.
The disciples in the company of the prophets at Jericho came to Elisha and asked, “Do you know that today the LORD will take your master away from you?” Elisha replied, “Yes, I know; be quiet.”

Elijah said to him, “Please stay here, for the LORD has sent me to the Jordan.” But Elisha replied, “As the LORD lives and as you yourself live, I will never leave you.” So the two of them went on together.
Fifty men from the company of the prophets also went and stood watching at a distance, while Elijah and Elisha stood by the Jordan.
Elijah took his cloak, rolled it up, and struck the water, and it parted to each side, so the two of them crossed over on dry ground.
After they had crossed over, Elijah said to Elisha, “Ask: before I am taken from you, what can I do for you?” Elisha answered, “Let me receive a double portion of your spirit.”
Elijah said, “You have asked a hard thing. But if you see me when I am taken from you, you will receive it; but if not, you will not.”
As the two of them were walking and talking, suddenly a chariot of fire and horses of fire appeared and separated the two of them, and Elijah went up to heaven in a whirlwind.
When Elisha saw it, he cried out, “My father, my father, the chariots and horsemen of Israel!” And he saw Elijah no more. So Elisha took hold of his own clothes and tore them into two pieces.
Elisha picked up the cloak of Elijah that had fallen, and went back and stood on the bank of the Jordan.
Elisha took the cloak of Elijah that had fallen and struck the water, and said, “Where is the LORD, the God of Elijah?” When he struck the water, it parted to each side, and Elisha crossed over.

When the disciples in the company of the prophets at Jericho who were watching saw this, they said, “The spirit of Elijah now rests on Elisha.” So they came to meet him and bowed down to the ground before him.

They said to Elisha, “Look, your servants have fifty strong men; please let them go and search for your master, in case the Spirit of the LORD has taken him up and set him down on some mountain or in some valley.” But Elisha replied, “Do not send anyone.”
But when they urged him until he was embarrassed, he said, “Send them.” So they sent fifty men, who searched for three days but did not find Elijah.
When they returned to Elisha, who was still at Jericho, he said to them, “Did I not tell you not to go?”
The men of the city said to Elisha, “Look, my lord, the city’s location is good, as you can see, but the water is bad and the land causes miscarriage.”
Elisha said, “Bring me a new bowl, and put salt in it.” So they brought it to him.
Elisha went out to the spring, threw the salt into it, and said, “Thus says the LORD: ‘I have healed this water; no longer will death or miscarriage come from it.’”
And the water has remained good to this day, according to the word that Elisha had spoken.
From there Elisha went up to Bethel, and as he was walking up the road, a group of youths came out of the city and jeered at him, “Go up, you baldhead! Go up, you baldhead!”
Elisha turned around and looked at them, and cursed them in the name of the LORD. Suddenly two female bears came out of the woods and mauled forty-two of the youths.
Elisha traveled from there to Mount Carmel, and from there he returned to Samaria.
